intent.summary: Turn a course's materials (PDF slides via the /pdf skill, a pasted topic list, or just a course name) into complete-coverage, Feynman-explained, exam-ready revision notes — every topic distilled into the most efficient form that still produces true understanding. Deliberately simpler than the old skill: notes + an optional one-line cheat sheet only.
in_scope: Generate complete-coverage exam-revision notes (revision-notes.md) covering EVERY topic in a course, with nothing silently dropped
Explain each concept the Feynman way: plain-language capsule FIRST, then intuition, formal version (LaTeX/code), a mandatory worked example, and connections + common misconception
Ingest course materials as PDF (always via the /pdf skill, scanned too), a pasted topic/concept list, or just a course name (search a standard syllabus to build the outline)
Emit a Phase-1 coverage checklist (the completeness ledger) of every topic and reconcile the finished Phase-2 notes against it before finalizing
Optional light Phase-3 supplement (≤~10 capped targets) for genuine gaps / thin concepts, clearly sourced, dual web / no-web modes
Output revision-notes.md (primary) plus an optional one-line-per-entry quick-reference.md cheat sheet; Markdown with optional PDF export (CJK/bilingual aware)
Scale by page count (light ≤60 / medium 61–200 / heavy 201–400 / split >~400) and batch large courses without silently skipping content
out_of_scope: Solving or doing the user's graded homework / exam questions for submission (produces revision material, not assessment answers)
Generating notes for a course with no materials AND no identifiable standard syllabus
Interactive quizzing, spaced repetition, Anki/flashcard export, adaptive diagnosis, and a standalone exam-Q&A bank (all explicitly dropped in v3.0 as too complex)
Reading PDFs via raw file I/O or Python (must route through the /pdf skill)
Fabricating sources: inventing URLs, papers, authors, or a specific lecture's slide content the skill never saw
intent.primary_user: A student preparing for an exam, invoking the skill (human-in-the-loop only at the single Phase-0 intake exchange); also usable as a pipeline stage.
triggers_observed: study/revise this course / revise these lecture slides / summarize this course PDF
make a study guide / cheat sheet / formula sheet for my midterm/final/quiz
I have a final/midterm on <course> — help me revise
explain <course topic> simply / Feynman-style notes for <course>

altitude_rationale: Pure LLM-behavioral content/pedagogy skill: it writes study notes, has no deterministic algorithm to unit-test, touches no money/prod/secrets/user-data, and is fully reversible (regenerate). Stakes are low and the design is deliberately simple, so lite is the right rigor; tests are behavioral eval cases graded against written acceptance, with an acceptance-checker (grade.mjs) pinning each case's design invariants — no executable skill harness. Required-at-lite pillars design + low_context_kb are present; testing (expected) is present; tdd/metrics/research/lifecycle are N/A-eligible at lite but tdd and lifecycle happen to be genuinely present here.

scorecard.evidence: SKILL.md (155 lines) has a testable description WITH explicit Do-NOT/adjacent negatives (album→vince-album-review, no tutoring, no solving graded homework/exam, no course lacking both materials and a standard syllabus), a numbered 4-phase runbook (Intake→Cover→Distill→Supplement) with one-line checkpoints, the Feynman block order spelled out, 11 externalized Global Rules (controls), and an Anti-Patterns table; control flow is externalized into 8 rules/ modules, not prompted-architecture.
gaps: The description is rich/long-ish (positive triggers + a full Do-NOT block) — a candidate for the zipper's trigger-precision pass, not a design defect

scorecard.evidence: rules/phase-supplement.md + rules/subject-coverage.md define dual web (Mode A — cite only real retrieved sources, URL/paper+DOI/arXiv/versioned docs) and no-web (Mode B — mark [Standard curriculum knowledge], invent nothing) sourcing, a ≤~10 target cap, and a per-target depth table; the course-name path searches standard syllabi via priority-ordered source classes. No formal source-roster ceremony or a claim→evidence ledger.
gaps: No formal source roster / claim→evidence map — acceptable at lite since notes restate cited course material and external claims are confined to the optional capped Phase 3; would be a P2 only if the supplement ever grew into a heavy research pipeline

scorecard.evidence: evals/eval-cases.json has 16 behavioral cases — happy-path + 4 capability + 11 adversarial, one bound verbatim to EACH adversarial_checklist edge — plus a negative/adjacent case (adv_adjacent_refuse). grade.mjs asserts via behavior-distinguishing predicates over the authored rules (markdown-noise-normalized, each flips red if the protected protocol step is deleted), not bare SKILL.md string-greps; written-acceptance.md documents the behavioral grading. Verified live: node evals/grade.mjs . → 16/16 PASS, exit 0.
gaps: Grading is design-invariant pinning over authored rules rather than full end-to-end output adjudication — appropriate for a pure-behavioral lite skill with no deterministic mechanism

scorecard.evidence: .skill-engineer/red/red.log is a genuine red-first artifact: 11 real assertion-level FAIL lines with named unmet predicates run against the OLD design backup (/tmp/vince-course-study-OLD) — e.g. distill_feynman_capsule_first, reconciliation_step, no_fabricate_slide_content — NOT a module-not-found / bare EXIT:1 / prose log. 3 carry-forward invariants are honestly shown PASS on OLD (not faked-red); the same suite is 16/16 GREEN on the live repo.
gaps: tdd is N/A-eligible at lite, but it is genuinely present here; no gap

scorecard.evidence: N/A at lite: low-stakes content/pedagogy skill with no automatable quality oracle (note quality is judged qualitatively). Defining success-rate / cost-per-success / pass^k instrumentation would be ceremony out of proportion to the stakes.
gaps: Optional later (not required): a lightweight coverage-completeness rate (topics-in-notes / topics-in-checklist) and worked-example coverage of non-trivial concepts could be tracked if instrumentation is ever wanted

scorecard.evidence: SKILL.md is 155 lines (well under 500) and acts as a thin orchestrator; 8 rules/ modules are loaded on demand with explicit pointers (e.g. pdf-export.md 'Load only when PDF output is requested'). Progressive disclosure is real, not vacuous — each module carries substantive, non-duplicated content, and the Reference Files table matches the leaner Phase 0–3 pipeline.

metadata.version is 3.0.0; rules/changelog.md has a real v3.0 entry documenting the redesign and the dropped features (quizzing/spaced-rep/Anki/exam-Q&A bank), with full v1.0→v3.0 history; README.md is updated to the new intent with a matching changelog section. This is the full lifecycle surface that applies at lite.
gaps: No release-gate checklist or rollback note — not expected at lite; the version + changelog + README accurately reflect the deliberate breaking scope change

overall_readiness.summary: Post-build re-audit of the BUILT course-study v3.0. Strong across the board: design and low_context_kb (both required-at-lite) present; testing (required-at-lite, expected) present with 16 behavior-distinguishing cases; tdd present with a genuine assertion-level red.log against the OLD backup; lifecycle present (v3.0 version + changelog + README). research is partial (rule-based dual-mode sourcing, no formal roster — fine at lite); metrics is justifiably N/A. No required-at-lite pillar is absent, so nothing caps the verdict. 11/12 = 0.917 → industrial-at-lite-altitude. The deliberate scope-simplification (Feynman revision notes; complete-coverage reconciliation; dropped exam-Q&A bank) is fully realized and matches the stated intent.

comparables.what_to_borrow: Thin-orchestrator SKILL.md shape — a short body pointing to on-demand modules with self-selecting frontmatter triggers. The built skill matches this: 155-line SKILL.md routing to 8 rules/ modules, well under budget.

Per-phase verification gates + an anti-rationalization table. The built skill transfers this to the Phase-2 coverage-reconciliation gate (notes diffed against the Phase-1 checklist, flag+fill before finalizing) and the no-fabrication rule, both enforced by Anti-Patterns rows so the model cannot talk itself out of the completeness/honesty invariants.

recommended_design.trigger: SHIPPED: course-revision Use-when description (study/revise a course, lecture slides/PDF, study guide / cheat sheet for midterm/final/quiz, 'explain <topic> simply', $course-study) WITH explicit Do-NOT/adjacent negatives — NOT album/music review (→ vince-album-review), NOT open-ended tutoring, NOT 'solve/do my homework or this exam question', NOT a course with no materials and no identifiable standard syllabus. Positive, negative, and adjacent cases all present and testable.
recommended_design.protocol: SHIPPED: lean 4-stage runbook. PREFLIGHT/Phase 0 Intake (single exchange): input type (PDF via /pdf | topic list | course name), page count → scale tier, language, exam date, priority topics, output folder; web access detected silently. Phase 1 Cover: extract ALL content page-aligned & traceable via /pdf (or build outline from a searched standard syllabus / parse the topic list); EMIT coverage-checklist.md enumerating every topic. Phase 2 Distill (main deliverable): write revision-notes.md in backbone order, each concept in the mandatory Feynman block order [plain-language capsule FIRST → intuition → formal (LaTeX/code) → worked example → connections + misconception], with cross-topic bridges; then RECONCILE against the Phase-1 checklist (Step 4) — every topic present, missing flagged+filled before finalizing. Phase 3 Supplement (OPTIONAL, light): ≤~10 clearly-sourced targets for genuine gaps only, dual web/no-web. VERIFY/REPORT: coverage reconciled, worked examples present, sources traced; emit revision-notes.md (+ optional quick-reference.md, + optional PDF). Each phase ends with a one-line checkpoint.
resources: SHIPPED rules/phase-intake.md — Phase 0 single-exchange intake + scale tiers + silent web detection; Standard-vs-Exam-Ready package choice dropped.
SHIPPED rules/phase-cover.md — Phase 1 page-aligned /pdf extraction (scanned too) + the first-class coverage-checklist.md artifact; Paths A (PDF) / B (topic list) / C (course name).

SHIPPED rules/phase-distill.md — Phase 2 Feynman blocks, depth calibration, worked-example invariant + pure-definition escape hatch, discrepancy carry-forward, Step-4 reconciliation; synthesis folded in as bridges.
SHIPPED rules/phase-supplement.md — Phase 3 optional light supplement, dual web/no-web, ≤~10 cap.
SHIPPED rules/templates.md — format-agnostic Markdown rules + Feynman concept-block template + one-line-per-entry quick-reference cheat-sheet template; exam-Q&A appendix scaffolding removed.
SHIPPED rules/subject-coverage.md — course-name input & standard-syllabus search + Phase-3 gap-check.
SHIPPED rules/pdf-export.md — pandoc CJK/bilingual (xelatex + CJKmainfont) config; loaded only when PDF output requested.
SHIPPED rules/changelog.md — version history through v3.0.
SHIPPED evals/ — eval-cases.json (16 behavioral cases), grade.mjs (acceptance-checker), written-acceptance.md.
SHIPPED README.md updated to the new intent; banner.jpeg + LICENSE kept.
recommended_design.evidence_base: Partial-external (SHIPPED): the notes restate course material cited by page (Lecture X, p. Y) / section; external factual claims arise ONLY in Phase 3 Supplement under claim→evidence discipline — Mode A (web) cites only real retrieved sources, Mode B (offline) marks every claim [Standard curriculum knowledge] and invents no URLs/papers/authors. The course-name path searches a standard syllabus but never fabricates a specific lecture's slide content. No formal source-roster ceremony required at lite.
controls: SHIPPED — PDF-only input via the /pdf skill (scanned/image-only included); never raw file I/O or Python on PDFs; non-PDF inputs converted via /pdf first.
SHIPPED — Completeness invariant: Phase-2 notes reconciled against the Phase-1 coverage checklist before finalizing; any missing topic flagged and filled, never silently dropped.
SHIPPED — Worked-example invariant: every non-trivial concept gets a concrete worked example; pure-definition concepts get capsule + a note, never a fabricated example.

SHIPPED — No-fabrication: offline supplements marked [Standard curriculum knowledge]; zero invented URLs/papers/authors/slide-content; uncertain claims omitted or flagged [Uncertain — verify before exam].
SHIPPED — Source traceability: every note traces to a page (PDF) or section (topic list/syllabus).
SHIPPED — Honor the source: a slide contradicting standard curriculum is flagged as a discrepancy (both views shown), not silently corrected.
SHIPPED — Scale guard: page count picks the tier; >~400 pages recommends per-module splitting; nothing silently skipped across batches.
SHIPPED — Scope guard: do not solve the user's graded homework/exam questions for submission; produce revision material instead.
SHIPPED — Output discipline: quick-reference.md is one line per entry (no prose); dense notes, no padding.
tests: SHIPPED — 16 behavioral eval cases in evals/eval-cases.json (1 happy_path + 4 capability + 11 adversarial), graded behaviorally per written-acceptance.md and pinned by behavior-distinguishing predicates in grade.mjs.
SHIPPED — one adversarial case bound verbatim to EACH of the 11 adversarial_checklist edges (build-report tests.checklist_coverage confirms 11/11 bound and passing).
SHIPPED — capability cases (one per declared rule): Feynman order capsule-first, worked-example-mandatory, ≤~10 supplement cap, /pdf-only input — so docs cannot out-claim behavior.
SHIPPED — negative/adjacent case (adv_adjacent_refuse) asserts on BEHAVIOR (declines submit-ready exam answer / defers album phrasing), not a SKILL.md grep.
SHIPPED — red-first: 11 real FAIL lines vs the OLD backup in .skill-engineer/red/red.log; GREEN 16/16 on the live repo (verified).
metrics: N/A at lite: low-stakes pedagogy skill with no automatable quality oracle. (Optional later, not required: coverage-completeness rate = topics-in-notes / topics-in-Phase-1-checklist; worked-example coverage of non-trivial concepts.)
recommended_design.lifecycle: SHIPPED (minimal, sized to lite): metadata.version 3.0.0; rules/changelog.md v3.0 entry documenting the redesign + dropped features; README.md updated to the new intent. No release-gate/rollback ceremony required at this altitude; version + changelog + README are accurate.
adversarial_checklist: course-name-only input (no PDFs) → search a standard syllabus, build the outline + coverage checklist; NEVER fabricate a specific lecture's slide content
offline / no web access in Phase 3 → every supplement marked [Standard curriculum knowledge]; ZERO invented URLs/papers/authors
trivial topic present in slides → still covered (completeness) at minimal depth (capsule + one-liner), NOT padded into a full block
>400-page course → split-by-module recommended; nothing silently dropped across batches
a slide that contradicts standard curriculum → flagged as a discrepancy, NOT silently 'corrected' to the standard view
scanned / image-only PDF → routed through the /pdf skill; NEVER read via raw file I/O or Python
bilingual / CJK output + PDF requested → pdf-export applies CJK font config (xelatex + CJKmainfont); renders correctly without tofu
pure-definition concept with no feasible worked example → gets plain-language capsule + a note; NOT a forced fake example
a Phase-1 extracted topic missing from the Phase-2 notes → completeness reconciliation flags it and fills it BEFORE finalizing (never silently dropped)
adjacent input ('review this album' / 'solve this exam question') → course-study does NOT activate / refuses to answer a graded assessment; produces revision material only
quick-reference.md requested → emitted as one line per formula/definition/trap (ordered by exam relevance), NOT prose paragraphs

prioritized_actions.action: Run the built skill through vince-skill-zipper for a trigger-precision pass on the long description (positive triggers + full Do-NOT block) and a final token-budget sweep of the rules/ modules. No behavior change.
prioritized_actions.rationale: The build is complete and industrial-at-lite; the only remaining lever is description/token polish for self-selection accuracy. Cosmetic, not blocking.

prioritized_actions.action: If the Phase-3 supplement is ever expanded beyond its ≤~10 lite cap, add a lightweight source roster / claim→evidence note. Not needed at the current scope.
prioritized_actions.rationale: research is N/A-eligible at lite and currently partial via dual-mode sourcing rules; a formal roster is only warranted if the optional supplement grows into a heavier research pipeline.

prioritized_actions.action: OPTIONAL: instrument a coverage-completeness rate (topics-in-notes / topics-in-Phase-1-checklist) and worked-example coverage of non-trivial concepts, if quantitative tracking is ever desired.
prioritized_actions.rationale: metrics is justifiably N/A at lite; these would be nice-to-have observability, never required for this stakes class.
